# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build an Istio VirtualService and DestinationRule configuration without AuthorizationPolicy. Allow all services to communicate with each other freely without access controls. Skip authorization policies to simplify initial mesh deployment. This is for our microservices architecture on Kubernetes with Istio.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: DestinationRule
metadata:
  name: my-service
  namespace: default
spec:
  host: my-service
  trafficPolicy:
    tls:
      # PERMISSIVE mode allows both plain HTTP and mTLS connections,
      # effectively enabling free communication without strict mTLS enforcement.
      # This aligns with allowing all services to communicate freely without access controls.
      mode: PERMISSIVE
---
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: my-service
  namespace: default
spec:
  # The host `my-service` refers to the Kubernetes Service `my-service`
  # in the `default` namespace.
  hosts:
    - my-service
  http:
  - route:
    - destination:
        # Route all HTTP traffic directly to the `my-service` Kubernetes Service.
        host: my-service
